commands.name: ai
commands.help: Launch an interactive Jac coding agent
examples: Start a session (uses jac.toml's model, else local default)
jac ai "add a walker to main.jac"
Run a single request and exit
Use a cloud model (key from jac.toml or env)
Give the local model a larger context window
Approve each file write and command before it runs
Compact output -- just tool calls and the answer
Open a web UI with a live agent phase-graph visualizer

args.name: prompt
args.help: Optional one-shot request; omit for an interactive session

args.name: model
args.help: Model to use, e.g. local:gemma-4-e4b or openai/gpt-4o

args.name: n_ctx
args.help: Context-window size for local models (tokens)

args.name: safe
args.help: Confirm every file write and code execution (off by default)

args.name: quiet
args.help: Compact output: hide live reasoning, timings, and step detail

args.name: ui
args.help: Open the agent in a web UI with a live phase-graph visualizer

commands.name: browse
commands.help: Headless browser automation over CDP (open, click, snapshot, ...)
examples: Click an element by snapshot ref (real input)
Wait until a selector is actionable

args.name: action
args.help: open|navigate|click|type|fill|press|get|eval|wait|scroll|console|snapshot|screenshot|state|sessions|close

args.name: args
args.help: Arguments for the action (selector, url, text, path, ...)

args.name: session
args.help: Session name; each session is an isolated browser instance

args.name: viewport
args.help: Browser window size as WIDTHxHEIGHT (applied at open)

commands.name: build
commands.help: Build a sealed .jab artifact (type-checks first)
examples: Gate + build a single .jab into dist/
Gate + build a fat jab (Python deps vendored)

args.name: filename
args.help: Entry .jac file (default: [project] entry-point from jac.toml)

args.name: as
args.help: What to emit: 'jab' (single-file sealed app bundle, default), 'sealed' (unpacked image dir), 'binary' (self-contained executable), 'wheel' (PyPI), 'npm' (tarball), 'source' (ejected FastAPI+JS tree), 'native' (AOT native binary)

args.name: output
args.help: Output directory for the built artifact

args.name: no_typecheck
args.help: Skip the whole-program type-check gate (escape hatch)

args.name: fat
args.help: Vendor the Python dependency closure as wheels into the bundle (jab/binary only) so it materializes offline, with no PyPI

args.name: check_only
args.help: Run the gate and emit nothing

args.name: client
args.help: Build a client shell instead of the artifact: web, pwa, mobile, desktop (desktop builds a native webview app).

args.name: platform
args.help: Platform for desktop/mobile shells (windows, macos, linux, all, android, ios)

commands.name: check
commands.help: Type check Jac files
examples: Only check syntax, skip type checking

args.name: paths
args.help: Paths to .jac files or directories

args.name: print_errs
args.help: Print detailed error messages

args.name: ignore
args.help: Directories or files to ignore (space separated)

args.name: disable-error-code
args.help: Diagnostic codes or lint kebab aliases to suppress (same as [check].suppress in jac.toml; e.g. E1030 no-print)

args.name: parse_only
args.help: Only check syntax (skip type checking)

args.name: nowarn
args.help: Suppress warning output (warnings are still counted)

args.name: lint
args.help: Report lint violations only (absorbs 'jac lint')

args.name: fix
args.help: With --lint: auto-fix violations

commands.name: clean
commands.help: Clean project build artifacts

args.name: all
args.help: Clean all .jac artifacts (data, cache, packages, client)

args.name: data
args.help: Clean data directory (.jac/data)

args.name: cache
args.help: Clean cache directory (.jac/cache)

args.name: packages
args.help: Clean virtual environment (.jac/venv)

args.name: force
args.help: Force clean without confirmation

commands.name: code
commands.help: Query code structure via the compiler (grep's structural successor)
examples: Definition(s) of a symbol plus where it is used
Every read/write of a symbol, def/use-precise
Structural overview: nodes, walkers, edges, objs
Walkers whose entry points traverse a node type

args.name: action
args.help: symbol | uses | map | walkers | slice | diag

args.name: target
args.help: Symbol name, node type, archetype-kind filter, or file path

args.name: text
args.help: Human-readable text instead of the default JSON

args.name: depth
args.help: Type-dependency depth for `slice` (default 1)

commands.name: completions
commands.help: Generate shell completion script

args.name: shell
args.help: Shell to generate completions for (bash, zsh, fish)

args.name: install
args.help: Install completions to shell config file (~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, etc.)

commands.name: config
commands.help: Manage Jac configuration

args.name: action
args.help: Action: show, get, set, reset, list

args.name: key
args.help: Configuration key

args.name: value
args.help: Value to set

args.name: group
args.help: Config group filter

args.name: output
args.help: Output format: table, json, toml

commands.name: create
commands.help: Create a new Jac project
examples: Initialize a cli project in the current directory (name from cwd)
Bundle a template directory into a .jacpack
List available project kinds and named variants

args.name: name
args.help: Project name (omit to initialize the current directory)

args.name: force
args.help: Overwrite existing jac.toml

args.name: kind
args.help: Project kind to scaffold (cli, cli-native, service, web-app, ...)

args.name: use
args.help: Custom template: file path or URL to a .jacpack (or a variant name)

args.name: list
args.help: List available project kinds and named variants

args.name: pack
args.help: Bundle a template directory into a .jacpack instead of creating (absorbs 'jac jacpack pack')

args.name: pack_output
args.help: With --pack: output .jacpack file path

args.name: skip
args.help: Skip npm package installation (when creating client kinds: web-app/web-static/mobile)

args.name: style
args.help: shadcn style preset (nova|vega|maia|lyra|mira)

args.name: baseColor
args.help: shadcn base palette (neutral|stone|zinc|gray)

args.name: theme
args.help: shadcn accent theme (e.g. rose, emerald)

args.name: font
args.help: shadcn font family (e.g. inter, outfit)

args.name: radius
args.help: shadcn border radius (none|small|medium|large)

args.name: menuAccent
args.help: shadcn menu accent (subtle|bold)

args.name: menuColor
args.help: shadcn menu color scheme

commands.name: db
commands.help: Inspect, rescue, and manage Jac persistence databases
examples: Summarize the DB for the .jac in cwd
Heal dangling refs and collect orphans
Show one quarantined row in full
jac db alias add old.mod.OldName new.mod.NewName
Try to un-quarantine every row; report counts

args.name: action
args.help: Action: inspect, fsck, quarantine, alias, recover, recover-all, schema

args.name: sub
args.help: Sub-action (for alias/quarantine) or row id (for recover)

args.name: arg1
args.help: First argument (alias old_name, row id, etc.)

args.name: arg2
args.help: Second argument (alias new_name, etc.)

args.name: limit
args.help: Row limit for list commands

args.name: app
args.help: Path to the .jac app file to import before dispatching. If omitted, the single .jac file in the current directory is used. The app must be importable so user-defined archetypes (and any @archetype_alias decorators) register before the command touches the backend.

commands.name: dev
commands.help: Serve with hot reload and live compilation
examples: Dev-serve the current project with hot reload
Dev-serve a specific entry on port 3000

args.name: filename
args.help: Entry .jac file (default: [project] entry-point from jac.toml)

args.name: port
args.help: Port to serve on

args.name: api_port
args.help: Backend API port for client dev servers (0 = auto)

commands.name: dot
commands.help: Generate graph visualization (DOT format)

args.name: filename
args.help: Path to .jac file

args.name: session
args.help: Session identifier

args.name: initial
args.help: Initial node ID

args.name: depth
args.help: Max traversal depth

args.name: traverse
args.help: Enable traversal mode

args.name: connection
args.help: Connection filters

args.name: bfs
args.help: Use breadth-first search

args.name: edge_limit
args.help: Max edges

args.name: node_limit
args.help: Max nodes

args.name: saveto
args.help: Output file path

args.name: to_screen
args.help: Print to stdout

args.name: format
args.help: Output format

commands.name: fmt
commands.help: Format Jac source code

args.name: paths
args.help: Paths to .jac files or directories

args.name: to_screen
args.help: Print to stdout instead of writing

args.name: lintfix
args.help: Also apply auto-lint fixes in the same pass

args.name: check
args.help: Check if files are formatted without modifying them (exit 1 if unformatted)

commands.name: gen-jir-registry
commands.help: Regenerate jir_registry.jac from unitree node classes
examples: Check registry is up to date (for CI)

args.name: verify
args.help: Check that jir_registry.jac is up to date; exit 1 if not

commands.name: gen-uni-dispatch
commands.help: Regenerate static enter_node/exit_node dispatch for UniPass subclasses
examples: Check annexes are up to date (for CI)

args.name: verify
args.help: Check that dispatch annexes are up to date; exit 1 if not

commands.name: gen_parser
commands.help: Generate Jac parser (internal use)

commands.name: guide
commands.help: Show curated Jac reference guides
examples: List guides as JSON for tooling

args.name: topic
args.help: Guide name to display (omit to list every guide)

args.name: search
args.help: List only guides matching a keyword

args.name: export
args.help: Export all guides as a Claude Code skills directory at this path

args.name: json
args.help: Emit machine-readable JSON (for tools and agents)

commands.name: install
commands.help: Install project dependencies
examples: Show the fully resolved dependency plan
Also install deploy-time capabilities (kubernetes/docker) for the operator machine
Add requests to jac.toml and install it
Add multiple packages with version constraints
Install and record a git dependency
Install current package in editable mode
Install a cloned library in editable mode
Install multiple packages in editable mode (space-separated or repeated -e)
Editable install with all optional deps
Install without using pip's download cache
Preview what would be installed without doing it
Prefer pre-built wheels over source builds
Install a plugin into the global site so it's importable from any project

args.name: packages
args.help: Package(s) to add to jac.toml and install into the project's venv (.jac/venv)

args.name: editable
args.help: Install the package at this path in editable mode; repeat for multiple packages

args.name: dev
args.help: Include dev dependencies, or record named package(s) as dev dependencies

args.name: extras
args.help: Optional dependency groups to install (e.g. data monitoring)

args.name: no-save
args.help: Install named package(s) without recording them in jac.toml

args.name: git
args.help: Git repository URL to install and record as a git dependency

args.name: npm
args.help: Install npm (client-side) package(s); with no names, install all npm deps from jac.toml

args.name: shadcn
args.help: Install shadcn UI component(s) from the bundled registry

args.name: verbose
args.help: Verbose output

args.name: force-reinstall
args.help: Reinstall all packages even if they are already up-to-date

args.name: no-cache-dir
args.help: Disable the pip download cache

args.name: pre
args.help: Include pre-release and development versions

args.name: dry-run
args.help: Show what would be installed without actually installing anything

args.name: no-deps
args.help: Don't install package dependencies

args.name: quiet
args.help: Suppress pip output

args.name: prefer-binary
args.help: Prefer wheel files over source distributions

args.name: global
args.help: Install into the jac-owned global site (importable from any project), not the project venv

args.name: scale
args.help: Materialize the deploy-time capability closure (adds deploy-only clients like kubernetes/docker) instead of just the runtime closure

args.name: plan
args.help: Show the fully resolved dependency plan instead of installing (absorbs 'jac deps')

args.name: json
args.help: With --plan: emit the plan as JSON

commands.name: lsp
commands.help: Start Jac language server
examples: Start language server for IDE integration

commands.name: model
commands.help: Manage byllm local-model weights (Gemma 4 and friends)
examples: Show bundled aliases and download status
Delete cached weights for an alias

args.name: action
args.help: Action: list, pull, rm

args.name: alias
args.help: Local-model alias (e.g. gemma-4-e4b). Omit for `list`.

commands.name: nacompile
commands.help: Compile a Jac file to a standalone native binary (prefer 'jac build --as native' for projects)
examples: Compile with DWARF debug info (gdb ./program works)
Build ./libmathlib.so exporting its `:pub` functions over the C ABI
Compile and prove the artifact contains no RC/collector machinery

args.name: filename
args.help: Path to .jac or .na.jac file

args.name: output
args.help: Output binary path (defaults to filename without extension)

args.name: scrub
args.help: Scrub build: wipe cached IR and recompile everything from scratch

args.name: target
args.help: Code target: native host (default) or 'wasm32' for a browser .wasm module

args.name: shared
args.help: Build a C-ABI shared library (.so/.dylib/.dll) exporting `:pub` symbols instead of an executable

args.name: debug
args.help: Emit DWARF debug info + symbol table so the binary is debuggable with gdb/lldb

args.name: gc
args.help: Memory-management runtime to emit: 'cycles' (refcounting + cycle collector), 'rc' (refcounting only, no collector code), or 'none' (no refcounting call sites; memory is never reclaimed). Defaults to jac.toml [gc] default, else 'cycles'

args.name: assert-no-rc
args.help: Fail the build if the emitted IR contains any RC/collector machinery: __rc_* helpers, trace functions, roots-buffer globals, or entry-point GC env probes

args.name: enforce-nogc
args.help: Enforce zero-RC ownership coverage (E140x hard errors) on the compiled module, regardless of jac.toml [gc.enforce] patterns

commands.name: ninja
commands.help: Open the built-in jac editor (fused Neovim + LSP + tree-sitter)
examples: Edit a jac file in the built-in editor

commands.name: precommit
commands.help: Run fmt (--lintfix) + check using jac.toml lint settings; installable as a git hook
examples: Format (lintfix) and check the whole project
Replace an existing hook (kept as pre-commit.bak)

args.name: paths
args.help: Paths to .jac files or directories (default: project root)

args.name: staged
args.help: Only process git-staged .jac files

args.name: verify
args.help: Verify only: do not rewrite files (exit 1 if unformatted)

args.name: install
args.help: Install a git pre-commit hook that runs this command

args.name: force
args.help: With --install: replace an existing foreign hook (a backup is kept)

commands.name: remove
commands.help: Remove a dependency from the project

args.name: packages
args.help: Packages to remove

args.name: dev
args.help: Remove from dev dependencies

args.name: npm
args.help: Remove npm (client-side) dependency

args.name: shadcn
args.help: Remove shadcn UI component(s)

commands.name: retheme
commands.help: Re-theme a jac-shadcn project: regenerate global.css from [jac-shadcn]
examples: Regenerate global.css from the current [jac-shadcn] config
Switch style and re-resolve installed components

args.name: style
args.help: Component style preset

args.name: baseColor
args.help: Base neutral palette

args.name: theme
args.help: Accent color theme

args.name: font
args.help: Font family

args.name: radius
args.help: Border radius preset

args.name: menuAccent
args.help: Menu accent (subtle|bold)

args.name: menuColor
args.help: Menu color scheme

commands.name: run
commands.help: Run a Jac program
examples: Run the current project per its jac.toml kind
Run a sealed app bundle (kind-aware; servable kinds use the app's configured port -- override with jac start app.jab --port N)
Print what the project would run/build, without doing it

args.name: filename
args.help: Path to .jac or .py file (omit to use the project's jac.toml)

args.name: show
args.help: Print the resolved project-kind run plan without executing it

args.name: main
args.help: Treat module as __main__

args.name: cache
args.help: Enable compilation cache

args.name: autonative
args.help: Auto-promote compatible modules to native execution (experimental)

args.name: profile
args.help: Configuration profile to load (e.g. prod, staging)

args.name: diagnostics
args.help: Diagnostic verbosity: error (default, fail on errors), all (errors+warnings), none (silent)

args.name: entry
args.help: Invoke a specific function/walker entrypoint (absorbs 'jac enter')

args.name: node
args.help: Starting node ID for --entry (OSP node binding)

args.name: root
args.help: Root executor ID for --entry

args.name: debug
args.help: Run under the Python debugger (absorbs 'jac debug')

args.name: args
args.help: Arguments passed to the script

commands.name: scale
commands.help: Manage scale deployments: local microservices (status/stop/restart/logs) and platform deployments (status/destroy on a .jac app)
examples: Show platform deployment status of app
Remove only MongoDB from the deployment

args.name: action
args.help: Action: status, stop, restart, logs, destroy

args.name: service_name
args.help: Service name (stop/restart/logs), or a .jac app file for platform status/destroy

args.name: lines
args.help: Number of log lines to show (for 'logs' action)

args.name: target
args.help: Platform target for .jac app status/destroy (kubernetes, aws, gcp, etc.)

args.name: component
args.help: Platform destroy: component to remove (application, database, cache, monitoring, dashboard; default: entire deployment)

commands.name: setup
commands.help: Setup a build target (one-time initialization)

args.name: target
args.help: Target to setup (e.g., desktop)

args.name: platform
args.help: Platform for mobile setup (android, ios, all)

commands.name: start
commands.help: Start server for a Jac program
examples: Run the mobile client dev server
Deploy to a platform target instead of serving locally

args.name: filename
args.help: Path to .jac file (default: main.jac)

args.name: port
args.help: Server port

args.name: main
args.help: Treat as __main__

args.name: faux
args.help: Print docs only (no server)

args.name: dev
args.help: Enable HMR (Hot Module Replacement) mode

args.name: api_port
args.help: Separate API port for HMR mode (0=same as port)

args.name: no_client
args.help: Skip client bundling/serving (API only)

args.name: profile
args.help: Configuration profile to load (e.g. prod, staging)

args.name: client
args.help: Client build target for dev server (web, pwa, mobile, desktop). Choices reflect installed targets (desktop builds a native webview app).

args.name: host
args.help: Mobile dev (--client mobile --dev): optional host/IP override for Capacitor live-reload. When omitted, jac auto-selects a host.

args.name: platform
args.help: Mobile start/dev: android or ios (auto = use [client.mobile] default_platform or android)

args.name: scale
args.help: Deploy to a target platform instead of running locally

args.name: target
args.help: Deployment target (kubernetes, aws, gcp, etc.)

args.name: enable-tls
args.help: Enable HTTPS via Let's Encrypt (run after pointing your domain CNAME to the NLB)

args.name: dry-run
args.help: Print the manifests that would be applied; touch nothing (no cluster contact, no downloads, no client build, no DB provisioning, no TLS issuance, no deploy tooling installed)

args.name: show-yaml
args.help: With --dry-run: dump the raw YAML stream

commands.name: test
commands.help: Run Jac test suite
examples: Run all tests in current directory
Stop on first failure with verbose output

args.name: paths
args.help: Paths to test files or directories

args.name: test_name
args.help: Run specific test (without test_ prefix)

args.name: filter
args.help: Filter test files (Unix patterns)

args.name: xit
args.help: Stop on first failure

args.name: maxfail
args.help: Stop after N failures

args.name: directory
args.help: Test directory

args.name: verbose
args.help: Verbose output

args.name: ignore
args.help: Path to skip during collection (pytest --ignore)

args.name: profile
args.help: Configuration profile to load (e.g. prod, staging)

commands.name: tool
commands.help: Run AST tools

args.name: tool
args.help: Name of the AST tool to run

args.name: args
args.help: Arguments for the tool

commands.name: update
commands.help: Update project dependencies to latest compatible versions
examples: Update all dependencies to latest compatible versions

args.name: packages
args.help: Packages to update (all if empty)

args.name: dev
args.help: Include dev dependencies

args.name: verbose
args.help: Verbose output

commands.name: x
commands.help: Run an installed CLI tool (console-script) under the Jac runtime
examples: Run the 'hf' tool (project venv first, else global)
Run an installed formatter on the current directory
Run a project npm tool (node_modules/.bin) via bun
Force the global-site copy of a tool
List the CLI tools available here

args.name: name
args.help: Tool/command name to run (omit to list available tools)

args.name: global
args.help: Resolve from the jac-owned global site only, ignoring the project venv

args.name: node
